disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden-onset difficulty understanding written words and word-finding difficulty (acute expressive language disturbance)
trigger_question_examples: What exactly happened when it started?
Are you having trouble finding or understanding words?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was reading an email and suddenly the words stopped making sense, and now I keep losing the words I want to say.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New acute confusion and disorientation during this episode
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel confused or muddled right now?
Do you know where you are and what time it is?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel really confused and foggy, like my thinking has switched off — I'm struggling to follow what's going on.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Abrupt onset a short time after starting screen/computer work, not resolving
trigger_question_examples: When and how quickly did this come on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It hit suddenly about half an hour after I got on the computer, and it hasn't eased off at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background of a viral illness months ago with persistent brain fog, memory problems and fatiguability
trigger_question_examples: Have you had anything like this before?
Have you been unwell or run down over recent months?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Since a bad virus a few months back I've had ongoing brain fog and memory trouble and I tire really easily, but nothing ever came on this suddenly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Caregiver-observable FAST motor screen currently negative — no facial droop, no one-sided arm or leg weakness, no slurred speech
trigger_question_examples: Is one side of the face drooping, or is an arm weak?
Is the speech slurred, or is it a problem finding the words?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Her face isn't drooping and her arms feel equally strong — it's not slurred speech, it's more that she can't find or understand the words and seems confused.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High distress in patient and spouse with fear of stroke or early dementia
trigger_question_examples: How are you both feeling about this?
What are you most worried it might be?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We're both terrified — my husband thinks I'm having a stroke and I'm scared it's early dementia.
